review_execution.command: cat prompt and /tmp/cp00-065-review.diff | claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json

parsed_review.overall_summary: CP00-065 'Synthetic Fixture Permission And Audit Binding' boundary suite is internally consistent across the contract definition, policy (CONTROL_PLANE_SYNTHETIC_FIXTURE_PERMISSION_AUDIT_BINDING_POLICY), module, assembler, validators, synthetic fixture, unit tests, and the RP00 contract validation script. It correctly binds Risk A units RP00.P05.M05.S01-S10, sets requested_pack_id=CP00-064 with a live-cursor correction to CP00-065, and intentionally leaves S11 for the next pack (source_micro_phase_completed=false, phase_completed=false, next_subphase=RP00.P05.M05.S11) exactly as the scope facts require. The assembler validates upstream CP00-064 pack identity and next_subphase=RP00.P05.M05.S01, fails closed to a 'blocked' decision with a coherent denied triplet when the upstream handoff is not bound, and rejects forbidden/unknown boundary claims; the result remains metadata-only with all runtime/write/permission-engine/audit-write flags false and no_real_data=true. Policy↔result field sets, acceptance gates, evidence refs (cp00-064), result_summary, construction_inspection, and README/test text markers all align with the validation script's expectations. Evidence files under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-065 are legitimately absent because this review precedes evidence creation. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues identified.
